title: Karatas morreniana
intro: Karatas morreniana, also known as Karatas, is a plant species native to the tropical regions of Central and South America, belonging to the family Apocynaceae. The Karatas plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, with reported health benefits including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects, which may help alleviate symptoms of fever, digestive issues, and respiratory problems. The bioactive constituents of Karatas morreniana include alkaloids, flavonoids, and phenolic compounds, such as vasicoline and vasicinone, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Traditionally, Karatas is used in herbal preparations, including infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are used to treat a range of health conditions, although modern usage and scientific validation of these preparations are limited.
